sut_env_keys.description: Environment variable names discovered from the SUT (keys only, never values).

stack_profile.description: Deterministic SUT toolchain detection (package manager, wrapper, install/test/start commands). Populated by src/qtea/stack_profile.py before the researcher agent runs.

url_resolution.description: Outcome of QA base-URL discovery (src/qtea/url_resolver.py). The chosen `key` is the env var the SUT's auth/sign-in code consumes; `value` is non-null when a literal default was recoverable.

sut_inventory.description: Language-agnostic, monorepo-aware introspection of the SUT(s). Produced by src/qtea/sut_inventory.py (Python AST + TS regex deterministic tiers) and augmented from the researcher agent's `## SUT Inventory` YAML block for any language the deterministic tiers don't cover. Consumed by Steps 7/8/9 to integrate generated tests with the SUT's existing layout, page objects, helpers, fixtures, and auth flow rather than ignore them.

custom_test_id_attribute.description: Non-default test-id attribute (e.g. data-test, data-cy). null means default data-testid.

architecture_pattern.description: Detected test-automation design pattern. Drives Step 8 lane selection: pom|inline|none|unknown → mature POM codegen lane; screenplay (and any other non-POM value) → exemplar-imitation lane.

pattern_exemplars.description: Verbatim source slices of the SUT's own reusable units (Screenplay Task/Question/Interaction etc.), captured for non-POM patterns so Step 8's exemplar lane generates new code by imitation. Empty for pom/inline/none.

src_directory_layout.description: Where the SUT puts production (non-test) code. Step 7 codegen places generated page objects + locators here, NOT under tests/.

lifecycle_hooks.description: Test setup/teardown hooks discovered in the SUT's own tests, classified by canonical trigger event so Step 7 can reuse them and Step 8 can regenerate them in the target framework's idiom. Populate for non-Python/TS stacks (Java @BeforeEach/@AfterEach, etc.) that Tier 1/2 can't scan. `calls` is the ordered list of calls in the hook body. Each item is EITHER an object `{method, args}` (preferred — captures argument expressions verbatim so Step 7 can replay them without arg-drop) OR a bare string like 'basePage.openBaseURL' (legacy shape — accepted for backward compatibility but args are unknown). New deterministic scanners always emit the object form; the LLM-augment merge normalizes strings to `{method, args: []}`.

event.description: Canonical trigger event. Map framework keywords: beforeAll/before/@BeforeAll/setUpClass/@fixture(scope=module) → before_all; afterAll/after/@AfterAll/tearDownClass → after_all; beforeEach/@BeforeEach/setUp/@fixture(scope=function,autouse) → before_each; afterEach/@AfterEach/tearDown/@fixture+yield → after_each.

file.description: SUT-relative path to the file defining the hook.

calls.description: Ordered calls in the hook body. Object form `{method, args}` preserves argument expressions verbatim (e.g. args=['MENU_ITEMS.HOME'] or ['USER','PASS']). Legacy string form (bare method name) is accepted for backward compatibility with pre-existing artifacts but carries no argument information.

oneOf.description: Legacy: bare method-call expression, e.g. 'basePage.logIn'. Args unknown.

method.description: Callee reference as source text, e.g. 'basePage.logIn'.

args.description: Ordered positional argument expressions, verbatim source text. Kwargs intentionally not captured (hook bodies use positional args).

navigation_preconditions.description: POM methods that act on a specific already-active view (grid/table/tab/filtered list) with no in-code guard, discovered to be consistently preceded by another call across the SUT's own real usages. Step 7 must plan `requires_call` before any reuse of `method`; Step 8 never sees this field directly. LLM-only (no deterministic scanner) — only emit when every real call site agrees and at least one is evidenced; never fabricate.

requires_call.description: The call that must happen first, as `ClassName.methodName` (e.g. 'BasePage.selectLoginOptionByText').

requires_args_hint.description: Optional hint for the argument the required call needs at real call sites (e.g. 'NAV_OPTIONS.DIRECTORY').

evidence.description: SUT-relative file[:line] of a real call site demonstrating the ordering, e.g. 'tests/EntityFormSmoke.spec.ts:104'.

existing_locators.description: Locator collections discovered in the SUT: constant classes (e.g. ChatPageLocators), object-literal bags (e.g. `export const BASE_LOCATORS = {...}`, including UPPERCASE_SNAKE names), inline class-property maps, readonly Locator properties, and modules of constants. MUST include bags that live in a SEPARATELY IMPORTED file (e.g. `<Pom>.locators.ts` imported into the POM) even when the POM has no inline locators — trace each POM's imports. Do NOT leave this `[]` when the SUT keeps locators in files/classes/constants: codegen relies on it to import/extend existing constants instead of inventing duplicates or dangling references. See prompt §8.

file.description: SUT-relative path to the file that DEFINES the bag/class/constants.

owning_pom.description: The POM this source belongs to (from a `<Pom>.locators.ts` filename or the POM that imports+uses it).

location_pattern.description: How codegen must write new constants into this source.

names.description: Optional: the constant/key names in the bag.

open_method.description: The page-object method that navigates to the app base URL (e.g. `page.goto('/')`), '<file>:<Class>.<method>'. This is the mandatory FIRST step of any UI test, before login. null when no such method exists in the SUT.

allOf.$comment: Non-POM patterns commit Step 8 to the exemplar lane, which imitates pattern_exemplars[] verbatim. A non-POM classification with zero exemplars has nothing to imitate — reject it.

dependency_warnings.description: Top-level imports in the SUT's test files that are not in the declared dependency set. Step 8 uses confidence=known entries to pre-install before the first pytest run; confidence=guessed entries surface as console warnings only.
